title: Hick's Law
summary: More options lead to harder decisions.
tip: Reduce choices in product flows to help users decide faster and more confidently.

title: Confirmation Bias
summary: People look for evidence that confirms what they think.
tip: Present multiple perspectives to challenge preexisting beliefs and broaden viewpoints.

title: Priming
summary: Previous stimuli influence users' decisions.
tip: Use carefully chosen visuals and language to shape user decisions.

title: Cognitive Load
summary: Total mental effort required to complete a task.
tip: Break information into clear chunks to lighten user effort.

title: Anchoring Bias
summary: Users rely heavily on the first piece of information.
tip: Lead with the most relevant information to guide decisions.

title: Nudge
summary: Subtle hints can affect users decisions.
tip: Use playful hints to encourage positive behaviors without force.

title: Progressive Disclosure
summary: Users are less overwhelmed if exposed to complex features later.
tip: Unveil advanced options only as users need them to prevent cognitive overload.

title: Fitts's Law
summary: Large and close elements are easier to interact with.
tip: Make primary buttons big and easy to reach for faster flows.

title: Attentional Bias
summary: Users' thoughts filter what they pay attention to.
tip: Guide focus with color, motion, or placement for key actions.

title: Empathy Gap
summary: People underestimate how much emotions influence behaviors.
tip: Account for user mood and motivation when planning flows.

title: Visual Anchors
summary: Elements used to guide users' eyes.
tip: Use shapes, images, or lines to guide visual attention.

title: Von Restorff Effect
summary: People notice items that stand out more.
tip: Highlight key actions with color, animation, or icons.

title: Banner Blindness
summary: Users tune out things they repeatedly see.
tip: Refresh key visuals regularly and avoid 'banner' placement for CTAs.

title: Decoy Effect
summary: Create a new option that's easy to discard.
tip: Add a less appealing choice to nudge users to your desired action.

title: Framing
summary: The way information is presented affects decisions.
tip: Frame messaging to emphasize benefits and value.

title: Selective Attention
summary: People filter out things from the environment when focused.
tip: Minimize distracting elements in key flows.

title: Survivorship Bias
summary: People neglect things that don’t make it past selection.
tip: Show both successes and failures to present a fair view.

title: Juxtaposition
summary: Elements close and similar are perceived as a unit.
tip: Group similar actions and fields for easy scanning.

title: Signifiers
summary: Elements communicate what they’ll do.
tip: Use icons or descriptive buttons to clarify actions.

title: Visual Hierarchy
summary: The order in which people perceive what they see.
tip: Size and color indicate importance—use to guide key actions.

title: Peak-End Rule
summary: People judge experiences based on their peak and end.
tip: Delight users at peak and end moments (e.g., onboarding and checkout).

title: Recency Effect
summary: Most recent items are remembered best.
tip: Place important information at the end of sequences.

title: Serial Position Effect
summary: First and last items are remembered best.
tip: Sequence CTAs and info so critical points are first and last.

title: Spacing Effect
summary: Learning is improved when information is spaced out.
tip: Use spaced repetition in notifications or reminders.

title: Zeigarnik Effect
summary: Unfinished tasks are remembered better.
tip: Show progress bars and checklists for motivation.

title: Chunking
summary: Grouping content aids memory and comprehension.
tip: Organize information into small, related groups.

title: Reminiscence Bump
summary: People recall events from adolescence and early adulthood better.
tip: Reference nostalgic moments in design for deeper engagement.

title: Mere Exposure Effect
summary: Familiar things are liked better.
tip: Use repeat branding and recurring elements for user comfort.

title: Forgetting Curve
summary: Memory fades rapidly without reinforcement.
tip: Remind users of key actions or content after initial use.

title: Picture Superiority Effect
summary: Images are easier to recall than text.
tip: Favor meaningful icons and illustrations.

title: Law of Prägnanz (Simplicity)
summary: People perceive complex images as simple forms.
tip: Design clean layouts with clear boundaries to enhance comprehension.

title: Gestalt Principle: Proximity
summary: Objects near each other are grouped together.
tip: Place related controls together for intuitive grouping.

title: Gestalt Principle: Similarity
summary: Similar objects are perceived as a group.
tip: Use shape and color to visually group related items.

title: Gestalt Principle: Continuity
summary: Elements arranged on a line or curve are perceived as flowing.
tip: Align elements for smooth viewing paths.

title: Gestalt Principle: Closure
summary: People perceive incomplete shapes as complete.
tip: Suggest boundaries to highlight clickable zones.

title: Gestalt Principle: Figure-Ground
summary: People distinguish objects from their background.
tip: Ensure good contrast between key content and surroundings.

title: Law of Common Fate
summary: Objects moving together are grouped together.
tip: Synchronize animation when grouping actions.

title: Law of Connectedness
summary: Connected elements are seen as a unit.
tip: Use lines or shapes to join related components.

title: Iconicity
summary: Icons communicate meaning quickly.
tip: Choose icons with clear, universal meaning for actions.

title: Metaphor
summary: People understand new concepts by comparison.
tip: Use familiar visual metaphors (trash can for delete) in UI.

title: Law of Diminishing Returns
summary: Each additional unit of effort yields progressively less benefit.
tip: Avoid overwhelming users with excessive steps or information in onboarding.

title: Reciprocity
summary: People feel obliged to return favors.
tip: Offer small, helpful features (like tips or free resources) to build goodwill.

title: Scarcity
summary: Limited availability increases value.
tip: Highlight limited features or time-based actions to drive urgency.

title: Authority Bias
summary: People trust advice from experts.
tip: Reference credible sources and endorsements in your content.

title: Social Proof
summary: People imitate what others do.
tip: Show positive testimonials and user counts to build trust.

title: Commitment and Consistency
summary: Past actions drive future behavior.
tip: Encourage small commitments, leading to stronger engagement over time.

title: Loss Aversion
summary: Losing feels worse than gaining feels good.
tip: Frame messaging to emphasize avoiding loss, not just gaining rewards.

title: Endowment Effect
summary: People value things they own more.
tip: Let users personalize, save, or unlock items to increase emotional connection.

title: Temporal Discounting
summary: People prefer immediate rewards over future ones.
tip: Offer instant gratification for actions when possible.

title: Peak Shift Effect
summary: People prefer exaggerated versions of familiar options.
tip: Make key CTAs bold or amplify signature design elements.

title: False Consensus Effect
summary: People overestimate how much others agree with them.
tip: Run usability studies to uncover differing perspectives and preferences.

title: Sunk Cost Fallacy
summary: People continue investing due to prior commitment, even when it’s irrational.
tip: Allow easy cancellation or reset options to prevent wasted effort.

title: Effort Justification
summary: Tasks that require effort are valued more.
tip: Let users customize, co-create, or win badges for their actions.

title: Hyperbolic Discounting
summary: People prioritize immediate rewards over larger future gains.
tip: Use instant feedback, rewards, or points to boost motivation.

title: Opportunity Cost
summary: People rarely consider what they’re giving up by making a choice.
tip: Show alternative benefits or costs to nudge better decisions.

title: Inattentional Blindness
summary: People miss what they’re not focused on.
tip: Reduce on-screen clutter and highlight changes in UI context.

title: Change Blindness
summary: People fail to notice changes when distracted.
tip: Use clear transitions and feedback for key screen changes.

title: Illusory Correlation
summary: People perceive a relationship where none exists.
tip: Show clear data and avoid suggestive pairings.

title: Planning Fallacy
summary: People underestimate the time tasks will take.
tip: Offer realistic estimates and progress updates.

title: Optimism Bias
summary: People believe they’re less likely to suffer negative outcomes.
tip: Use gentle reminders and realistic messaging about risks.

title: Choice Overload
summary: Too many choices can lead to indecision and frustration.
tip: Limit available options or help users quickly filter choices.

title: Default Effect
summary: People are likely to stick with preset options.
tip: Set helpful defaults for common actions or selections.

title: Negativity Bias
summary: Negative events are weighted more heavily than positive ones.
tip: Address negative feedback rapidly and celebrate wins.

title: Endowment Progress Effect
summary: Small initial progress increases motivation.
tip: Show starter progress bars or pre-filled tasks to motivate action.

title: Foot-in-the-Door Technique
summary: Small requests increase likelihood of compliance with larger ones.
tip: Start with simple actions, then gradually ask for more commitment.

title: Door-in-the-Face Technique
summary: Refusing a large request increases chance of accepting a smaller one.
tip: Begin with big asks, then offer the realistic alternative you really want.

title: Hot-Cold Empathy Gap
summary: People struggle to predict their own behavior in different emotional states.
tip: Design for real-world emotions, not idealized rational use.

title: Affect Heuristic
summary: Decisions are strongly influenced by current emotions.
tip: Foster positive feelings with delightful microinteractions and copy.

title: IKEA Effect
summary: People value things more when they help create them.
tip: Let users personalize or customize elements of your product.

title: Testing Effect
summary: Retrieving information helps strengthen memory.
tip: Encourage users to quiz themselves or recap main points for longer retention.

title: Pygmalion Effect
summary: High expectations lead to improved performance.
tip: Set positive goals and use uplifting language in your UI.

title: Placebo Effect
summary: Belief in benefit can lead to real improvement.
tip: Frame instructions and feature releases in positive, confident language.

title: Cognitive Dissonance
summary: People feel discomfort when holding conflicting beliefs.
tip: Use clear messaging and consistent visuals to minimize confusion.

title: Self-Serving Bias
summary: Successes are attributed to self, failures to externals.
tip: Celebrate users’ accomplishments, offer encouragement for setbacks.

title: Availability Heuristic
summary: People rely on immediate examples when evaluating options.
tip: Use recent case studies, screenshots, and testimonials for clarity.

title: Temporal Contiguity
summary: Events that happen together are linked in memory.
tip: Pair feedback with actions in real time for stronger association.

title: Backfire Effect
summary: Counter evidence can reinforce prior beliefs.
tip: Guide patiently and avoid confrontational messaging.

title: Own-Race Bias
summary: People better recognize faces of their own race.
tip: Test with diverse imagery and personas.

title: Halo Effect
summary: Positive impressions spill over into unrelated areas.
tip: Use attractive visual design to boost feature credibility.

title: Bystander Effect
summary: People are less likely to help when others are available.
tip: Make call-to-action responsibilities clear and personalized.

title: Social Loafing
summary: Group settings reduce individual effort.
tip: Recognize individual contributions and encourage sharing in teams.

title: Groupthink
summary: Desire for harmony suppresses alternative viewpoints.
tip: Enable dissent and invite feedback for group decisions.

title: Spotlight Effect
summary: People overestimate how much others notice them.
tip: Normalize mistakes and foster a supportive community.

title: Fundamental Attribution Error
summary: Overemphasize personality, underemphasize situation.
tip: Offer context and encourage empathy for user actions.

title: Just-World Hypothesis
summary: People believe everyone gets what they deserve.
tip: Prevent bias in feedback, highlight fairness and diversity.

title: False Memory
summary: People remember things inaccurately.
tip: Offer clear logs, undo features, and confirmations.

title: Mood-Congruent Memory
summary: Current mood biases recall of similar memories.
tip: Use positive tone and image choices to favor pleasant recalls.

title: Serial Recall
summary: People remember sequences in the order presented.
tip: Guide users step by step for best recall in flows.

title: Method of Loci
summary: Associating information with familiar places aids memory.
tip: Use location-based metaphors for organizing content.

title: Contrast Effect
summary: Judgments are impacted by comparison to previous items.
tip: Use visual contrast to make key actions stand out.

title: Projection Bias
summary: People think future preferences will mirror present ones.
tip: Gather updated feedback regularly—don't rely only on initial surveys.

title: Illusion of Transparency
summary: People overestimate how well others can understand them.
tip: Use explicit wording and tooltips for clarity.

title: Time Perception
summary: Time feels shorter when focused and longer when bored.
tip: Break long tasks into stimulating subtasks or gamify progress.

title: Temporal Patterning
summary: Memory is boosted by rhythmic or patterned presentation.
tip: Use animation or repetition for onboarding flows.

title: Stage Bias
summary: Early or late stage information gets more attention.
tip: Begin and end flows with key content for stronger impact.

title: Completion Bias
summary: People prefer tasks they can complete.
tip: Favor quick wins and progress indicators that reach 100%.

title: Deadline Effect
summary: Urgency increases focus and performance.
tip: Set time limits for key actions or reward timely flows.

title: Surprise Effect
summary: Unexpected events capture attention.
tip: Add delightful Easter eggs or microinteractions for engagement.

title: Peak Shift Principle
summary: Users prefer exaggerated versions of familiar options.
tip: Make key CTAs or elements bold and memorable.

title: Availability Bias
summary: Recently encountered ideas dominate decision-making.
tip: Show recent uses or examples to reinforce desired actions.

title: Schema Theory
summary: People organize knowledge into mental frameworks.
tip: Match navigation to users' expected mental maps.

title: Prospect Theory
summary: People value gains and losses differently.
tip: Highlight net positive outcomes and avoid risk language.

title: Primacy Effect
summary: First impressions weigh heavier.
tip: Ensure your opening screens are polished and welcoming.

title: Misattribution of Arousal
summary: Emotional states influence unrelated decisions.
tip: Design calming flows for high-stakes or stressful steps.

title: Status Quo Bias
summary: Users prefer things to remain unchanged.
tip: Offer upgrades or improvements as easy, no-risk options.

title: Appraisal Theory
summary: Emotion is shaped by subjective interpretation of events.
tip: Guide user reactions through positive framing and uplifting feedback.

title: Self-Efficacy
summary: Belief in one's ability boosts motivation.
tip: Use affirmations, encouragement, and clear progress markers.

title: Social Facilitation
summary: Presence of others improves performance on simple tasks.
tip: Gamify with leaderboards or public achievements for easy wins.

title: Social Inhibition
summary: Presence of others decreases performance on complex tasks.
tip: Let users practice privately before sharing or competing.

title: Echo Chamber Effect
summary: Environments reinforce shared beliefs.
tip: Promote diverse content and support constructive debates.

title: Authority Effect
summary: Advice from credible sources is easily accepted.
tip: Leverage expert endorsements and trusted badges.

title: Gradient Effect
summary: Users naturally follow gradual progression.
tip: Use color gradients or stepwise guidance in processes.

title: Flashbulb Memory
summary: Vivid memories form from emotionally charged events.
tip: Mark important milestones with memorable visuals and notifications.

title: Heuristic Thinking
summary: People use mental shortcuts for quick decisions.
tip: Standardize choices, automate frequent actions, and highlight key routes.

title: Implicit Bias
summary: Unconscious attitudes affect understanding and actions.
tip: Audit design and language for neutrality.

title: Latent Inhibition
summary: Familiar stimuli are less noticeable.
tip: Use fresh visuals or microcopy to highlight updates.

title: Misattribution Effect
summary: Users link feelings or ideas to the wrong source.
tip: Clarify cause/effect and make feedback direct.

title: Prototype Effect
summary: Typical examples are remembered better than atypical ones.
tip: Focus on core/common use cases in feature design.

title: Anchoring Effect
summary: People give undue weight to the first piece of information.
tip: Start with the most important fact or CTA.

title: Framing Bias
summary: Decisions are affected by how information is presented.
tip: Frame options with positive and clear wording.

title: The Dunning-Kruger Effect
summary: People with low ability overestimate competence.
tip: Provide gentle feedback, guides, and paths for self-improvement.

title: Frequency Illusion (Baader-Meinhof)
summary: Seeing something once makes it seem suddenly everywhere.
tip: Repeat important messages for desired visibility.

title: Perceptual Set
summary: Expectations shape perception.
tip: Preview flows and clarify expected results up front.

title: Context Effect
summary: Surroundings influence interpretation.
tip: Match imagery and copy to the user’s task and state.

title: Hindsight Bias
summary: Events seem predictable after they happen.
tip: Document reasoning and decisions for reflection.

title: Misinformation Effect
summary: Memory can be altered by exposure to misleading information.
tip: Always provide clear notifications, confirmations, and records.

title: Position Effect
summary: Placement impacts selection; extremes are more likely to be chosen.
tip: Arrange important choices at the beginning or end of a sequence.

title: Self-Reference Effect
summary: Information related to oneself is better remembered.
tip: Personalize messages and let users attach their name or avatar.

title: Frequency Principle
summary: Repeated exposure increases recall and familiarity.
tip: Remind users of features or tasks as needed.

title: Spacing Principle
summary: Breaks between repetitions improve learning retention.
tip: Use spaced repetition for onboarding and reminders.

title: Interference Effect
summary: Similar information can distract or overwrite memory.
tip: Keep instructions simple and avoid presenting unrelated details.

title: Encoding Specificity Principle
summary: Context at learning improves recall.
tip: Match cues (color, icon, copy) for reminders and confirmation dialogs.

title: Overconfidence Effect
summary: People overestimate their own ability, skills, and judgment.
tip: Give gentle nudges to validate, review, and check decisions.

title: Goal Gradient Effect
summary: Motivation increases as people get closer to a goal.
tip: Show countdown, motivational messages, and 'Almost there!' prompts.

title: Restorative Effect
summary: Pauses or breaks restore attention and improve engagement.
tip: Suggest rest breaks for long tasks or learning modules.

title: Congruence Principle
summary: Consistency of visual and verbal cues aids memory and trust.
tip: Keep UI labels, instructions, and feedback in harmony.

title: Distinctiveness Effect
summary: Distinct items are more memorable.
tip: Make your primary CTA pop with color, shapes, and motion.

title: Subjective Contour Effect
summary: Brain fills in missing parts of visual information.
tip: Use suggestive shapes and whitespace for elegant design.

title: Dual Coding Theory
summary: Information presented in multiple formats is easier to learn.
tip: Combine text with icons or diagrams in explanations.

title: Retroactive Interference
summary: New information can disrupt recall of previous content.
tip: Use confirmations and reminders to reinforce earlier learning.

title: Proactive Interference
summary: Old information hinders learning of new content.
tip: Use clear onboarding and quick feedback to anchor new behaviors.

title: Semantic Encoding
summary: Meaningful content is easier to remember.
tip: Tie new concepts to real-life use cases.

title: Elaboration Principle
summary: More detailed mental processing improves learning.
tip: Offer deeper dives and 'learn more' links for interested users.

title: Transfer of Learning
summary: Knowledge from one area helps in another.
tip: Draw analogies and reference familiar scenarios for new features.

title: Capacity Limits
summary: Short-term memory holds about 7 items.
tip: Chunk information and keep screens simple and focused.

title: Subitizing
summary: Quick, accurate judgment is easier for small quantities.
tip: Display less than five items for instant comprehension.

title: Repetition Effect
summary: Repeating information boosts memory.
tip: Echo key ideas and actions at crucial moments.

title: Sleep Effect
summary: Sleep strengthens new memories.
tip: Encourage meaningful breaks and healthy habits.

title: Neglect of Probability
summary: Risk is overlooked unless made explicit.
tip: State odds, risks, and consequences clearly for transparency.

title: Barnum Effect
summary: Vague, general statements are accepted as personally meaningful.
tip: Personalize onboarding, updates, and suggestions using user context.

title: Illusion of Control
summary: People believe they can influence uncontrollable outcomes.
tip: Offer toggles and customized controls to foster engagement.

title: Information Overload
summary: Too much info impairs decision-making.
tip: Keep flows minimal and prioritize essential content.

title: Redundancy Principle
summary: Simple redundancy (e.g., text + icons) improves comprehension.
tip: Echo key actions with icons, labels, and feedback.

title: Attribution Theory
summary: People seek explanations for events and behaviors.
tip: Clarify cause, effect, and context in feedback and notifications.

title: Conjunction Fallacy
summary: People assume specific conditions are more probable than general ones.
tip: Simplify choices and clarify probabilities.

title: Regression to the Mean
summary: Extreme cases tend toward average over time.
tip: Show average statistics and normalize benchmarks.

title: Similarity Effect
summary: Similar items are grouped and selected together.
tip: Use color-coding or icons to group related features.

title: Temporal Context Effect
summary: Timing and order affect how events are perceived.
tip: Order onboarding steps logically and with consistent timing.

title: Reciprocal Teaching
summary: Learning is enhanced by alternating roles.
tip: Let users share tutorials or tips with peers.

title: Mental Models
summary: Users apply existing frameworks to new experiences.
tip: Match navigation patterns to familiar apps and flows.

title: Trial-and-Error Learning
summary: Multiple attempts lead to better solutions.
tip: Let users experiment, undo actions, and celebrate improvement.

title: Explicit Learning
summary: Direct instruction speeds up new skill acquisition.
tip: Provide step-by-step guides and clear tooltips.

title: Implicit Learning
summary: Learning happens unconsciously through exposure.
tip: Use repetition and ambient guidance for subtle teaching.

title: Jakob's Law
summary: Users spend most of their time on other sites.
tip: Users prefer your site to work the same way as all the other sites they already know.

title: Trust Bias
summary: People are more likely to trust systems that appear reliable.
tip: Use professional design and clear language to build trust.

title: Doherty Threshold
summary: Productivity soars when computer and user interact at a pace (<400ms) that ensures neither has to wait on the other.
tip: Provide system feedback within 400ms to keep users engaged.

title: Feedback Loop
summary: Continuous feedback helps users adjust their actions.
tip: Provide immediate feedback for user actions to guide behavior.

title: Recognition Over Recall
summary: It's easier to recognize things than recall them from memory.
tip: Minimize the user's memory load by making objects, actions, and options visible.

title: Gamification Facilitation
summary: Game-like elements increase engagement and motivation.
tip: Use points, badges, and leaderboards to encourage desired behaviors.

title: Social Presence
summary: The sense of being with others in a virtual environment.
tip: Use avatars and real-time indicators to create a sense of community.

title: Tesler's Law
summary: Every application has an inherent amount of complexity that cannot be removed.
tip: Ensure that the complexity is handled by the system, not the user.

title: Occam's Razor
summary: The simplest solution is usually the correct one.
tip: Remove unnecessary elements to reduce complexity.

title: Postel's Law
summary: Be conservative in what you do, be liberal in what you accept from others.
tip: Accept variable input from users, but provide consistent output.

title: Reward Bias
summary: People are motivated by the promise of a reward.
tip: Offer tangible or intangible rewards for completing tasks.
